publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-02-04 23:59:11 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Graduate Women in Science (GWIS), formerly known as Sigma Delta Epislon, is an international professional organization for women in science established as a scientific women's fraternity in 1921 at Cornell University. The Sigma Delta Epsilon records (1922-2024) contain historical and founding materials, memorabilia, correspondence, newsletters and bulletins, membership lists and individual member files, histories of Sigma Delta Epsilon Graduate Women in Science and its chapters, and reports, minutes, and tape recordings from national meetings and conventions.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Graduate Women in Science (GWIS), formerly known as Sigma Delta Epsilon, is an international professional organization for women in science. It was established as a scientific women's fraternity on May 21, 1921, at Cornell University by Adele Lewis Grant. It was founded as a fraternity for women pursuing graduate degrees in the sciences. In 1922, a similar local group for women at the University of Wisconsin-Madison agreed to merge with Sigma Delta Epsilon, establishing a national fraternity. Its purpose was "to further interest in science, to provide a fraternity for the recognition of women in science, and to bring them together in a fraternal relationship."

Sigma Delta Epsilon was incorporated in the state of New York in April 1922. It held its first national conference on April 20, 1922. It joined the American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) as an associate member in 1936 and as an affiliate member in 1939. In 19701, the fraternity changed its name to Sigma Delta Epsilon Graduate Women in Science, and again to Graduate Women in Science (GWIS) in April 2016.

Graduate Women in Science offers grants, awards, and fellowships. It serves an international network of women scientists and promotes the participation and representation of women in science-related events. The GWIS National Meeting is held annually in June. It also sponsors mentoring, webinars, and seminars featuring its members' research. As of 2024, the society publishes four electronic newsletters on successive weeks in each month: GWIS Connect, GWIS Lead, NEWS DIGEST, and the member-only GWIS Empower.

Membership in the Graduate Women in Science is open to anyone with at least a bachelor's degree in a STEMM discipline (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics, Medicine) or equivalent professional experience.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Sigma Delta Epsilon records (1922-2024) contain historical and founding materials, correspondence, newsletters and bulletins, membership lists and individual member files, histories of Sigma Delta Epsilon Graduate Women in Science and its chapters, and reports and minutes from national meetings and conventions. Also inlcudes financial records, memorabilia, and publications by SDE-GWIS members. Also, tape recordings of Grand Chapter, National Council, and interim meetings.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Sigma Delta Epsilon records, #3605. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Sigma Delta Epsilon, Alpha Chapter records, #37-4-835.

did.unittitle: Series I. Historical, Governance, and Policy materials
Subseries Ia. General Historical, Governance, and Policy Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For the 2024 Bylaws, see box 1 folder 60.

did.unittitle: Articles of Incorporation 1922, Amendment to Article 1971, Title IX regulation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summary lists of all SDE-GWIS histories, elected officers, honorary members, meeting dates and locations, all winners of fellowships and grants-in-aid, all awardees of Meritorious Service Award, all Hartley Corp Donations (to 2023), and 2023-2024 SDE-GWIS Leadership

did.unittitle: Subseries Va. Sigma Delta Epsilon News and Sigma Delta Epsilon Bulletin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original bulletin was titled Sigma Delta Epsilon News until it was changed to the Sigma Delta Epsilon Graduate Women in Science Bulletin in 1971 and was published under this name until it ceased publication in 2015.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Name changed to Sigma Delta Epsilon Graduate Women in Science Bulletin from this volume until it ceased publication in 2015.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the summer of 1969 (Vol. 1) through 1979 (Vol. IX) Newsletters were published in addition to Bulletins. Purpose of Newsletters as to fill a communication gap between spring and fall publications. However, this schedule was changed during those years. When Helen Haller became Editor, she incorporated the numbering system into the Bulletin numberings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No. 1 missing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: E-News and Information was published by various leaders roughly monthly from December 2006 to August 2008.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2015 (4 issues; began in September); 2016 (12 issues); 2017 (1 issue, final; January 2017, succeeded by the newsletters below). Also includes 2 e-Newsletters from June and July 2015 that predated the Broadcaster

Monica J. McCann, "Influence of Central Nervous System on Gastric Function"

Rose Marie Belforti, "Familial and Subfamilial Phylogenetic Relationships within the Malvales"
Karine Schaal, "Energy Avail & Bone Turnover in Intense Training"
Maria Grace Calinato, "Nitrite Reductase Activity in Myoglobin"

scopecontent.p: Includes photos of Ariel Hollinshead, Katherine Jaouni, Gladys Pigman, Nell Hornstein, and Nell Mondy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For end of tape 6/tr-21922, see tr-21923.

did.unittitle: National Council and Grand Chapter meetings, and the Conference in College Park, MD
Today's Women in Science, By Lambda Chapter (Los Angeles)
WISE Outstanding Service Award to Graduate Women in Science from WISE Institute at Penn State University
Sweatshirt, white, with SDE logo and wording
T-shirt, purple, from 2002 National Meeting, Washington, DC
T-shirt, green with figures and words on front, from 2011 annual meeting (90th Anniversary), Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Two T-shirts from the 2022 Conference in Madison, WI (100th Anniversary Meeting); one gray, one navy blue; both have GWIS and the new logo on the front, and pictures on the back
Set of 15 souvenir pins from the 2015 Conference in Sioux Falls, SD
Eloise Gerry's Honorary membership badge, engraved with her name and date

"This Is What A Scientist Looks Like" containing six AVI-format video clips (1/2 to 1 min each) of women from different GWIS chapters, introducing themselves, what work they do and where, and ending "…and this is what a scientist looks like". Made at the Ithaca Annual Meeting, June 2011.
Elizabeth Weisburger rough cut of feature "Great Lives of Women Scientists", oral history project
Framed SDE Insignia, gold leaf with black Greek lettering
Proclamation of Women in Science Week in Massachusetts
Outstanding Student Organization of the Year Award to Penn State Chapter plaque
Certificate Establishing Chapter at Iowa State College
"Cartoon Humor Book for Working Women, "J.P. Kilbourn
"The Book For Working Women," J.P. Kilbourn
"A How to Book Making Success Your Reality, "J.P. Kilbourn
"History of Paleontological Research Institute (1932-1982)," Katherine V.W. Palmer
"Margaret Pittman: Down to a 40-hour work-week," Gail McBride, Bulletin of Medical Alumni Association, Medicine on the Midway, University of Chicago Spring 1987

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Award from SDE January 1986 to Margaret Pittman, see page 8 of article

did.unittitle: "Women in Science: Why so few?" Alice S. Rossi, Reprint from Science, May 28, 1965 Vol. 148, No. 3674 Pages 1196-1202
"Women Scientists: Problems and Prospects," Alice S. Rossi, Paper delivered to conference on Women in Science. The Wisconsin Center, Madison Wisconsin,
"Vi and Mam: The lives of Viola Louise Sommermeyer and Carrie Murray, " Karl H. Sommermeyer
"American Women in Science Before The Civil War," Elizabeth Wagner
"Science Is For Girls: Portraits of Women Succeeding in Science," Ed. Anne Weber-Main
"Writing About Science," Joy Frestedt, Anne Weber-Main, and Kimerly Wilcox
"Home-Made" Histories of Women in Science, compiled by Nu Chapter
"Women's Health Research Resource Booklet," Center for Women's Health and Women's Health Research, University of Wisconsin
The Odyssey of a Women Field Scientist: A Story of Passion, Persistence, and Patience, Jean H. Langenheim
Profiles of Pioneer Women Scientists, Elizabeth Moot O'Hern, Ph.D.
Sigma Delta Epsilon: Graduate Women in Science website

daodesc.p: Sigma Delta Epsilon: Graduate Women in Science website [Delivery]
